articles.headline: Humanoid Robots Learn Dexterous Manipulation from Human Video
articles.digest: Researchers at MIT have developed a framework that enables humanoid robots to learn complex manipulation tasks directly from unlabelled human demonstration videos. The system uses a novel cross-embodiment transfer technique to bridge the morphological gap between human hands and robot end-effectors. In lab trials, robots trained solely on YouTube cooking videos achieved a 78% success rate on unseen utensil-handling tasks.

articles.headline: Boston Dynamics Deploys Spot Fleet for Autonomous Infrastructure Inspection
articles.digest: Boston Dynamics announced a commercial partnership with a major utility provider to deploy a 50-unit Spot fleet for fully autonomous pipeline and substation inspection. Each robot runs a custom SLAM stack built on top of ROS 2 Nav2, with onboard LiDAR-camera fusion providing centimetre-accurate localisation in GPS-denied environments. The deployment represents the largest known commercial robotics fleet operating without human supervisors in real infrastructure.

articles.headline: Open-Source Foundation Model Achieves State-of-Art on RoboManip Benchmark
articles.digest: A team from UC Berkeley released RoboFM-7B, a 7-billion parameter vision-language-action model that outperforms proprietary baselines on the RoboManip benchmark by 12 percentage points. The model is trained on a new dataset of 1.2 million robot trajectories collected across 14 labs worldwide using a standardised data-collection protocol. Weights, training code, and the full dataset are released under Apache 2.0, marking a significant open-science milestone for robot learning.
